objective: Make the first public release operable after deployment by defining health checks, smoke evidence, monitoring signals, rollback switches, log boundaries, and incident response before external users or store reviewers rely on the app.
releasePrinciples: Do not launch publicly without a known rollback path.
Do not enable real paid video generation until worker health, storage persistence, and provider quota are verified.
Do not log API keys, passwords, full cookies, private customer uploads, or unredacted personal data.
Treat public health, authenticated smoke, PWA install, account deletion, storage persistence, and worker queue health as separate release gates.
Keep the first release conservative: limited concurrency, review-safe account, dry-run fallback, and daily monitoring during week one.

healthSurfaces.currentEvidence: public:check verifies the public health endpoint returns ok=true and status=ready
mustNotShow: internal filesystem paths beyond safe operational summaries

healthSurfaces.currentEvidence: public:check and pwa:public-smoke verify public root, support, legal pages, manifest, and PWA assets
blocked until review-safe account is available
mustShow: runtime config endpoint hides private keys
mustNotShow: real passwords in logs or repository

healthSurfaces.url: private worker health endpoint or cloud provider dashboard
healthSurfaces.currentEvidence: blocked until libTV worker and persistent storage are configured
mustShow: last successful dry-run or real generation
failed job reason is visible without secrets
mustNotShow: private uploaded source files in public logs

monitoringSignals.frequency: daily during week one, then release cadence
daily during week one and after auth changes
after deploy, after restart, and daily during week one
daily during week one after real generation is enabled
daily during week one and weekly after launch

rollbackPlaybooks.trigger: Public root or PWA shell fails to load after deploy.
rollbackActions: Rollback to previous static dist/container artifact.
Keep public legal/support pages available.
Run npm run public:check after rollback.
Record failed release version, rollback time, and impacted surfaces.
verification: mobile screenshots baseline still reflects usable UI

rollbackPlaybooks.trigger: Login fails, sessions disappear after refresh, or credentialed CORS is rejected.
rollbackActions: Restore last known good PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN and CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS.
Restore last known good CONSOLE_AUTH_COOKIE_SECURE and CONSOLE_AUTH_COOKIE_SAMESITE.
Keep CONSOLE_AUTH_REQUIRED=true.
Run npm run production:auth-plan and authenticated npm run cloud:smoke.

rollbackPlaybooks.trigger: Real video generation fails, queue grows, provider errors spike, or cost/quota is abnormal.
rollbackActions: Set LIBTV_DEFAULT_DRY_RUN=true.
Reduce BATCH_MAX_WORKERS to 1 or 2.
Keep uploads, prompt generation, and dry-run flows available.
Record failed provider, error type, queue impact, and cost impact.
verification: No new real generation jobs are submitted while paused

rollbackPlaybooks.trigger: Uploads, generated outputs, review seed data, or data-rights records disappear after restart.
rollbackActions: Stop public expansion and store submission.
Restore RUN_STORAGE_DIR to the last known persistent volume path.
Recover files from backup if available.
Run upload, dry-run, export, and deletion-request smoke tests.
verification: Account export does not expose secrets
Deletion requests remain visible to admins

rollbackPlaybooks.trigger: Privacy page, support page, or account deletion request flow becomes unreachable or inaccurate.
rollbackActions: Pause store submission and public marketing.
Restore last known good legal/support page build.
Keep a monitored support contact available.
Run npm run compliance:check and npm run public:check.
verification: Privacy, terms, AI disclosure, support, and delete-account pages load

logSafetyRules: Never log plaintext passwords or password hashes.
Never log private uploaded source files or customer assets in public logs.
Redact Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, and provider secret headers.
Keep data-rights and deletion-request logs limited to request id, status, operator, and timestamps.
If a log sample is attached to a store review or support ticket, remove account identifiers and private file paths first.

response: Stop rollout or pause public promotion.
Notify operator/legal owner if privacy or deletion is affected.
Reduce worker concurrency or enable dry-run fallback.
Run public and authenticated smoke tests after mitigation.
Update screenshots and docs when the user-facing behavior changes.

requiredEvidence: npm run public:check passes against https://www.zkraiflow.top
authenticated npm run cloud:smoke passes with a review-safe account
health endpoint reports ready and authRequired=true
Previous build/container rollback action is documented
Log safety rules are accepted by engineering
Support/store evidence logs are redacted before sharing

doNotCommit: Raw production logs containing user data
